item.description:
This is a post with executable code. border-bottom

Output appears here…

Best Cards

Output appears here…

item.title: buttonRow
item.description:
Best canonical method, very clean OJS input & buttons, boostrap-styled and filled in-a-row.

// static DIV container with .button-row class that will programmatically trigger effective row and children styling
container = html`<div id="row_container" class="button-row">
placeholder container text will be overwritten
 </div>`
viewof RefInput = {
  const inputText = Inputs.text({
  value: "Gen1:1",
  style: "width: 80px;",
  })
  return inputText
}

// YIELD BOOSTRAP STYLING via classes added to its <input> element
restyl = {
 const IS = viewof RefInput.querySelector('input');
 if (IS) {
    IS.classList.add("form-control", "form-control-lg");
  };
};
viewof btnA = Inputs.button("Store");
viewof btnB = Inputs.button("Clip");
viewof btnC = Inputs.button("Help");

// SIDE-EFFECT DOM operations
// 
result = {
  const midiv = document.querySelector("#row_container");
  midiv.replaceChildren(viewof RefInput, viewof btnA, viewof btnB, viewof btnC);
}
// RESTYLE OJS viewof buttons
restyle = {
  // APPLY STYLE to PARENT button-row container
  const mirow = document.querySelector("div.button-row");
    mirow.style.display = "flex";
    mirow.style.width = "100%";
    mirow.style.gap = "3px"; // between children spacing

 const AS = viewof btnA.querySelector('button.btn.btn-quarto');
 if (AS) {
    AS.classList.add('btn-warning');
    AS.classList.remove('btn-quarto');
  };
  
  const HB = viewof btnB.querySelector('button.btn.btn-quarto');
  if (HB) {
    HB.classList.add('btn-info');
    HB.classList.remove('btn-quarto');
  }
  
  const CB = viewof btnC.querySelector('button.btn.btn-quarto');
   if (CB) {
//     CB.style.color = "black";
     CB.classList.add('btn-success');
     CB.classList.remove('btn-quarto');
//   addmib.style.cssText = "color: white; background-color: red;";
  }

// this is inoperative here !? let mini_styles.css do it 
/*  mirow.querySelectorAll("button").forEach(btn => {
    btn.style.flex = "1";
    btn.style.margin = "20px";
  });
*/  
}

NB Quarto Bootstrap buttons

item.title: MirrorFull
item.description:
This document shows you how to use a basic example Svelte component called Circles.svelte.

Create one using an HTML template:

myMirrorReset = html`<my-mirrorfull></my-mirrorfull>`

item.title: quarto-svelte example
item.description:
This document shows you how to use a basic example Svelte component called Circles.svelte.

Create one using an HTML template:

myCircles = html`<my-circles></my-circles>`

Now the fun part - updating our visual when our document changes. It’s as simple as changing the prop using componentName.propName =.

For example, here are three datasets and an Observable Inputs dropdown menu that lets you select one of three datasets:

// here are some datasets...
allDatasets = new Map([
    ["Dataset A", [5, 15, 25, 17, 8]],
    ["Dataset B", [25, 5, 5]],
    ["Dataset C", [12, 5, 8, 21, 5]]
  ]);
viewof selectedDataset =
  Inputs.select(allDatasets, { label: "Selected dataset" });

Now we can update the prop data of the visual myCircles using:

myCircles.data = selectedDataset

And there you go! 🚀

For more help writing Svelte components, check out the Svelte tutorial or take look at some of the examples in the quarto-svelte documentation.

item.title: MirrorReset
item.description:
This document shows you how to use a basic example Svelte component called Circles.svelte.

myMirrorReset = html`<my-mirrorreset></my-mirrorreset>`

item.title: MirrorIndex
item.description:
This document shows you how to use a basic example Svelte component called Circles.svelte.

Create one using an HTML template:

myMirror = html`<my-mirror></my-mirror>`

item.title: ButtonsUp
item.description:
HTML Buttons Naked (no-attributes, no-styling)

HTML Buttons with .btn.btn-primary (yields Bootstrap styling)

HTML Output: placeholder output area

item.title: Buttons
item.description:
HTML Output:

What is happening in this file:

  • HTML Input/Button: These are “dumb” elements. They do nothing until the htmlBtn.onclick function in the OJS block manually pulls the value from the input and pushes it into the span.
  • OJS Input (Inputs.text): This is “smart.” Because of the viewof keyword, the variable ojs_text is globally available to the OJS runtime.
  • OJS Button (Inputs.button): Unlike the HTML button, this one returns a value (an incrementing integer). By simply mentioning ojs_btn in a code block, you tell OJS to re-run that specific block every time the button is pressed.

HTML Output: 

htmlBtn = document.getElementById('h-btn');
htmlInput = document.getElementById('h-in');
htmlOutput = document.getElementById('h-out');
htmlBtn.onclick = () => {
  htmlOutput.innerText = htmlInput.value;
};
viewof ojs_text = Inputs.text({
  label: "OJS Input"
})

// This button yields a value (the number of clicks)
viewof ojs_btn = Inputs.button("OJS Button")

// --- OJS Reactive Output ---
// This markdown block updates automatically whenever ojs_text changes
md`OJS Text Value: ${ojs_text}`
{
  ojs_btn; // Referencing the button makes this block depend on it
  return md`OJS Button was clicked!`;
}

item.title: MirrorGemini
item.description:
This document shows you how to use a basic example Svelte component called Circles.svelte.

Create one using an HTML template:

myMirror = html`<my-mirrorgemini></my-mirrorgemini>`
